# synix

> A build system for agent memory with declarative pipelines, incremental rebuilds, and full provenance tracking

## What it does

Synix transforms raw conversations into searchable, hierarchical memory artifacts through declarative Python pipelines. Define layers (transcripts → episodes → rollups → core memory), transforms, and grouping strategies — change a config, only affected layers rebuild. Every artifact traces back through the full dependency graph to its source conversations.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output (transcript, episode, rollup, core memory) with SHA256 content addressing
- **Layer** — named level in memory hierarchy forming a DAG (level 0 = sources, higher levels = more abstract)  
- **Pipeline** — Python definition of layers, transforms, grouping strategies, and projections
- **Transform** — processing operation (parse, episode_summary, monthly_rollup, topical_rollup, core_synthesis)
- **Projection** — materializes artifacts into usable outputs (SQLite FTS5 search index, markdown context doc)
- **Provenance** — complete chain from any artifact back to source conversations, always included in search results
- **Fingerprint-based caching** — SHA256 of inputs + prompt + model config determines rebuild necessity

## Installation and quick start

Install via pip or uvx:
```bash
pip install synix
# or
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project
uvx synix build pipeline.py
uvx synix search "topic"
```

Initialize with existing exports:
```bash
uvx synix init my-project --source ./exports/chatgpt.json
```

## CLI commands

- `synix init <name>` — scaffold new project with sample pipeline
- `synix build [pipeline.py]` — run pipeline, only rebuilds changed artifacts  
- `synix plan [pipeline.py]` — show what would build without executing
- `synix plan --explain-cache` — detailed cache decision breakdown per layer
- `synix search <query>` — full-text + semantic search across indexed layers
- `synix search <query> --layers episodes,core` — restrict search to specific layers
- `synix search <query> --trace` — include full provenance chains in results  
- `synix show <artifact-id>` — display artifact content (resolves by label or ID prefix)
- `synix lineage <artifact-id>` — show complete provenance tree
- `synix list [layer]` — show all artifacts, optionally filtered by layer
- `synix validate` — run configured validators against artifacts
- `synix verify` — check build integrity (hashes, provenance, search index consistency)
- `synix clean` — remove build directory

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── cli.py                  # Click CLI commands
├── pipeline/
│   ├── config.py          # Parse Python pipeline → Pipeline objects  
│   ├── dag.py             # DAG resolution, build order, rebuild detection
│   └── runner.py          # Execute pipeline, walk DAG, cache artifacts
├── artifacts/
│   ├── store.py           # Filesystem-backed artifact storage
│   └── provenance.py      # Provenance tracking and lineage queries
├── transforms/
│   ├── base.py            # Transform interface
│   ├── parse.py           # Source parsers (ChatGPT/Claude JSON → transcripts)
│   ├── summarize.py       # LLM transforms (episode, rollup, core synthesis)
│   └── prompts/           # Prompt templates as text files
├── projections/
│   ├── search_index.py    # SQLite FTS5 materialization and querying
│   └── flat_file.py       # Render core memory as markdown context doc
├── sources/
│   ├── chatgpt.py         # ChatGPT export parser  
│   └── claude.py          # Claude export parser
└── search/
    └── embeddings.py      # Embedding generation for semantic search
```

## Pipeline definition

Define memory architecture in Python:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Layer, Projection

pipeline = Pipeline("personal-memory")
pipeline.source_dir = "./exports" 
pipeline.llm_config = {"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "temperature": 0.3}

pipeline.add_layer(Layer(name="transcripts", level=0, transform="parse"))
pipeline.add_layer(Layer(name="episodes", level=1, depends_on=["transcripts"], 
                        transform="episode_summary", grouping="by_conversation"))
pipeline.add_layer(Layer(name="monthly", level=2, depends_on=["episodes"],
                        transform="monthly_rollup", grouping="by_month"))  
pipeline.add_layer(Layer(name="core", level=3, depends_on=["monthly"],
                        transform="core_synthesis", grouping="single"))

pipeline.add_projection(Projection(name="memory-index", projection_type="search_index",
                                  sources=[{"layer": "episodes", "search": ["fulltext"]},
                                          {"layer": "core", "search": ["fulltext"]}]))
```

## Important constraints

- **SQLite only** — no external databases required, filesystem + SQLite FTS5 for search
- **No web UI** — CLI-first tool, outputs flat files and SQLite database  
- **Local execution** — runs on your machine, your API keys, your data
- **Source file formats** — ChatGPT JSON, Claude JSON, plain text/markdown with YAML frontmatter
- **LLM providers** — OpenAI native + any OpenAI-compatible API via base_url override
- **Python 3.11+** — uses modern Python features, uv-native development workflow
